title: Try, Try Again
lesson: If you fall down, you can get back up and try again.
prompts: Place the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image onto a sunny park path — green grass, leafy trees, warm sunlight — sitting on a little red balance bike with both feet on the ground, wearing a snug safety helmet. The person from the input image is on the far left, facing the camera in an eye-level medium shot with their face fully visible and an excited smile. {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} stands to the right, one hand resting gently on the bike, smiling encouragingly. Photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

Place the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image onto a sunny park path — green grass, leafy trees, warm sunlight — wobbling on the little red balance bike, wearing the safety helmet, leaning slightly to one side with a surprised expression. The person from the input image is on the far left, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible in an eye-level three-quarter shot. {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} stands to the right, reaching out a hand, looking watchful and ready to help. Photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

Show the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image on a sunny park path — green grass, leafy trees, warm sunlight — after a gentle tumble, sitting safely on the green grass beside the little red balance bike, still wearing the safety helmet, with a tiny pout but no tears. The person from the input image is on the far left, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible in an eye-level medium shot. {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels to the right, leaning in kindly and offering an open hand. Photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

Place the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image onto a sunny park path — green grass, leafy trees, warm sunlight — standing back up next to the little red balance bike, wearing the safety helmet, brushing off the grass with a determined little smile. The person from the input image is on the far left, facing the camera in an eye-level medium shot with their face fully visible. {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} crouches to the right, clapping and cheering them on warmly. Photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

Place the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image onto a sunny park path — green grass, leafy trees, warm sunlight — riding the little red balance bike a short way, wearing the safety helmet, beaming with delight, both feet gliding just above the ground. The person from the input image is on the far left, head and torso turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible in an eye-level three-quarter shot, not a back view. {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} stands further to the right in the background, cheering with both arms raised. Photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

Show a warm resolution on a sunny park path — green grass, leafy trees, golden afternoon light: the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands proudly beside the little red balance bike, one hand resting on the handlebar, wearing the safety helmet, with a big happy smile. The person from the input image is on the far left, facing the camera in an eye-level medium shot with their face fully visible. {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels to the right, cheering and giving a thumbs-up with a proud, delighted expression. Photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
texts: You sat on your little red bike, helmet on tight. "I'm going to ride!" you said. Dad smiled beside you.
You pushed off — but wibble, wobble! The bike tipped to one side. "Whoa!" you cried.
Down you went onto the soft green grass. It didn't hurt, but you felt a little sad.
Dad held out his hand. "You can try again," he said kindly. You stood up and brushed off the grass.
You pushed off once more and — you were riding! "Look at me go!" you laughed. Dad cheered with his arms up high.
You stood proudly by your bike with a great big grin. "You did it!" said Dad. You never gave up.
